name: Marriage & Children
epithet: The du'as for the home you are building
title: Marriage & Children Du'as: the supplications for marriage and your children in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
meta_description: Du'as for the newly married, before intimacy, congratulating a newborn, and seeking Allah's protection for your children, in Arabic, transliteration and English with sources.
keywords: marriage dua, dua for newly married, dua before intimacy, dua for a newborn, congratulating a newborn in Islam, protection for children, dua for children, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: A home does not begin with the keys or the furniture. It begins with a few words handed up to Allah: bless this, keep the shaytan out of it, let what grows here be good. The Prophet ﷺ gave the believers exactly those words, for the day two people are joined, for the night they come together, and for the children Allah trusts to their care.

These are the supplications of the family. Say them over a new marriage, before intimacy, over a newborn laid in your arms, and over the children you would protect with your life. You are asking the One who gave them to you to guard what you cannot.
intro: Match the du'a to the moment. Number 190 is what you say to a couple just married. Number 191 is the husband's du'a over his wife at the start. Number 192 is said quietly before intimacy. Numbers 145 and 146 are for a newborn, the words of congratulation with their reply, and the Prophet's ﷺ own seeking of refuge for his grandchildren.

Recite the Arabic, lean on the transliteration while it is still new on your tongue, and read the meaning so your heart knows what you are asking for. None of these are long. Said with presence, each is the work of a breath.
closing: Marriage and children are among the heaviest trusts Allah gives, and the most beloved. The sunnah does not leave you to carry them alone. It hands you words for the joining, words for the bed, words over the cradle, and a shield of refuge for every child you raise. Say them, and keep saying them, and let the home you are building rest in hands that never tire.

O Allah, bless what You have joined and what You have given, keep the shaytan far from our spouses and our children, and let every soul under our roof be a coolness to our eyes and a people of Paradise.

faq.q: What do you say to congratulate someone on a new baby in Islam?
faq.a: The transmitted words ask Allah to bless the gift, to let the new parents give thanks, and to let the child reach maturity in righteousness (du'a 145). The parent then replies, asking Allah to bless and reward the one who congratulated them. It is a far richer exchange than a simple 'congratulations'.

faq.q: Is there a du'a to say before intimacy with my spouse?
faq.a: Yes. The Prophet ﷺ taught that if a husband says 'Bismillah, O Allah keep the shaytan away from us and from what You provide for us' (du'a 192) before intimacy, then if a child is decreed from that union, the shaytan will never be able to harm it. Say it quietly between yourselves.

faq.q: How did the Prophet ﷺ seek protection for children?
faq.a: He would seek refuge for his grandsons al-Hasan and al-Husayn in the Perfect Words of Allah from every devil, every harmful creature, and every envious eye (du'a 146). It is a sunnah you can keep over your own children, by day or at night.

email.subject: The du'as for the home you are building
email.alt_subject: What the Prophet ﷺ said over a new marriage and a newborn
email.preview: Words for the joining, the night, and the children, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

A home does not begin with the keys or the furniture. It begins with a few words handed up to Allah: bless this, keep the shaytan out of it, let what grows here be good. The Prophet ﷺ gave the believers exactly those words, for the day two people are joined, for the night they come together, and for the children Allah trusts to their care.

We have gathered the du'as of the family, each with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source. There is the du'a for a couple just married, the husband's du'a over his wife, the quiet supplication before intimacy, the words of congratulation over a newborn with their reply, and the very refuge the Prophet ﷺ sought for his own grandchildren.

Marriage and children are among the heaviest trusts Allah gives, and the most beloved. The sunnah does not leave you to carry them alone.

The full collection, formatted to read or recite, is here:

social.hook:
A home doesn't begin with the keys.

It begins with a few words handed up to Allah.
social.caption:
The du'as for the home you are building.

The sunnah gives you words for every part of the family: a blessing over a new marriage, the husband's du'a over his wife, the quiet supplication before intimacy, the words of congratulation over a newborn, and the very refuge the Prophet ﷺ sought for his own grandchildren, al-Hasan and al-Husayn.

Marriage and children are among the heaviest trusts Allah gives, and the most beloved. You don't carry them alone.

Save this, and read or recite the full collection with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/marriage-children

#MarriageDua #DuaForChildren #MuslimFamily #MuslimMarriage #SunnahDua #Dhikr #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

145.title: Congratulating new parents (and the reply)
145.note: The words to greet a new birth: a prayer that Allah bless the gift, that the parents give thanks, and that the child reach maturity in righteousness. The parent then replies, asking Allah to reward and bless the one who came to congratulate them.

146.title: Seeking refuge for a child
146.note: The Prophet ﷺ would say this over his grandsons al-Hasan and al-Husayn, seeking their protection in the Perfect Words of Allah from every devil, every harmful creature, and every envious eye. A shield to keep over your own children.

190.title: For the newly married
190.note: What you say to a couple just married: that Allah bless each of them, pour His blessing over them, and join them together in goodness. The du'a to greet a new marriage with.

191.title: A husband's du'a over his wife
191.note: Taught for the start of a marriage: asking Allah for the goodness in her and the goodness she was created upon, and seeking refuge from any evil in her or in her nature. A du'a of trust at the very beginning.

192.title: Before intimacy
192.note: Said quietly between husband and wife. The Prophet ﷺ taught that if a child is decreed from a union begun with these words, the shaytan will never be able to harm it.
